trajectory.description: Observation: v25 test shows one episode with 17 safe steps and 33 detector steps, but other episodes 0 safe with very high collisions (up to 1502) indicating the robot gets stuck colliding repeatedly. Avg safe low at 3.4. Policy reaches human sometimes but OA logic fails leading to stuck behavior when detector drops or near obstacles/human. Analysis: High collision episodes suggest OA is either not triggering properly or the turn direction is wrong leading to pushing against obstacle instead of turning away. Detector gating helps but not sufficient. Early versions had higher avg safe (~50), so current tuning regressed. The dynamic nature requires better speed matching (~0.25 normalized vel) and tighter angle control for facing while moving. The manual safe count and JSON are working. Decision: Update to v26 with refined OA (higher min_front threshold 0.75, stronger backup+turn with tested sign, gate OA stricter on detector<0.15), improved follow mode (base_linear=0.28 for human match, err_gain=0.18 for dist correction clipped to [0.1,0.45], pure rotate threshold 0.4 rad, ang_k=-6.5, seek mode for dist>3.0 with higher linear=0.7). Add collision accumulation tracking per epi. Make code complete without any truncation. Run test_full_episode to check if collisions reduced and safe/streak increased (target max_streak >100, avg_safe>30). Then submit_eval on the improved predict.py with dummy ckpt v26. This should improve the primary metric.

trajectory.description: Observation: v26 test completed but all eps have 0 safe_steps (max_streak=0), avg_safe=0, high collisions in some eps (up to 399), detector varies but safe condition never met despite detector=425 in one epi; eval also 0.0 nav_seek_success/safe_follow_steps, avg_dist~5.8m. Earlier v4-v5 reached ~50-100 safe. Analysis: OA triggers on human (depth sees humanoid as obstacle when close/detector high) causing turns away or stuck pushing (high collisions); follow mode not maintaining dist~1.5m + |angle|<0.2618 simultaneously for accumulation on dynamic oracle; speed match 0.28 + gains cause drift/overshoot. Root cause: OA not sufficiently gated by detector, loose pure-rotate threshold, imperfect err correction for persistent facing+dist. Reactive heuristic viable per literature (~0.5 follow-rate). Decision: rewrite FULL complete non-truncated test_full_episode.py + predict.py + train.py with clean v27 HeuristicPolicy (OA strictly gated detector<0.25+min_front<0.9, turn-to-open-side using left_d vs right_d with correct sign, backup if dist<0.9 or min_front<0.6, follow on detector>0.1|dist<3.5 with base=0.26+0.18*err clipped[0.1,0.45], pure-rotate if |angle|>0.4 with linear=0, stronger ang_k=-7 to -8, seek linear=0.75 when far, robust depth with wider center 35-65% + sides 30% + valid>0.05, per-step safe+streak+max_streak+detector+coll accum using exact config angle+post-step compass, improved get_collision_delta, full JSON with make_serializable+all keys+manual safe>=500 for success+metrics fallback, abs paths+dataset+concur_render=False). Run test_full_episode.py (5 eps) to validate reduced collisions + higher safe/streak (>100 target in good epi). Then submit_eval on predict.py with updated dummy ckpt v27. This is final push for positive nav_seek_success within remaining 3 iters/5.8h.
